content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (iter-057)

## TOP PRIORITY — bridge 3 is the single live tensor-flank blocker
- **`pullbackTensorMap_left_unitality` (L1244, the genuine geometric crux).** Closing it closes the entire
  S4b flank (it propagates: bridge 3 → `hbr` in `tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner` → S4b body, all
  already proven mod this one sorry). It is B1-scale (sheafification-boundary reconciliation of sheafified-δ
  legs against `congrArg a_Y.map hlu`, threading bridge 1 η-identification + `pullbackValIso`/`sheafifyUnitIso`/
  `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` naturality + `sheafificationCompPullback`/counit squares; RHS leg `f^*(λ_M)` needs
  `pullbackValIso` naturality against `(tensorObj_left_unitor M).hom`).
- **ACTION = EFFORT-BREAK, not a blind prove round.** The planner's OWN reversal signal (iter-056 plan.md)
  pre-designates this: a compiling partial exists (bridge-2 rewrite + spine `hlu`), so the prove-pass-stalled
  condition is met. Decompose into: (a) the δ-mate identification leg, (b) the η-mate identification leg
  (bridge 1 is already the seed), (c) the unitality assembly against `hlu`. Do NOT re-dispatch the same
  whole-lemma prove recipe — it is multi-hundred-LOC by analogy to B1.
- The spine seed `have hlu` at L1236 is currently dead (unused before the sorry) — the effort-breaker's
  sub-lemmas should consume it; the next prover removes it if the decomposition routes around it.

## SECONDARY — Cone B (dual flank S3/S4a), scheduled iter-057
- **S3 `dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat` (L1099)** + **S4a `dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat` (L1123)** — NOT
  attempted this iter (planner deferred to land Cone A first / avoid a 2nd speculative lane on a CHURNING
  route). Both need the bespoke internal-hom base-change cone: generalize `presheafDualUnitIso_naturality` to
  the immersion `j`. There is NO Mathlib dual-preservation API for `f^*`. Blueprint Cone B blocks already
  exist (planner wrote them iter-056). Recommend: dispatch blueprint-reviewer to HARD-GATE the Cone B chapter,
  then a prove lane only if PASS. Co-dispatch with the bridge-3 effort-break is fine (root is frozen/green —
  no parallel-lane build race; both are SOLO-safe in the terminal file but they write the SAME file, so
  serialize prover work or run one lane).

## GATED — do not assign yet
- **`trivialisation_restrict_compat` (L1501)** — telescope of all 5 squares; transitively gated on bridge 3 +
  S3 + S4a. Independent of bridge 3 in dependency terms (auditor confirmed L1501 ⊥ L1244) but needs ALL five
  square lemmas closed before it can assemble. Keep deferred until S4b + Cone B land.

## Hygiene (next prover on this file — non-blocking)
- **Iter-number drift** (auditor major): `exists_tensorObj_inverse` docstring references iter-206/225/226+
  (prior project lifecycle). Refresh to current counter.
- **Missing `[IsOpenImmersion f]`** on bridge lemma signatures (auditor minor) — harmless but inconsistent
  with the rest of the file's convention.
- **Cosmetic**: long-line linter at L1219/L1482/L1496; deprecated `Sheaf.val` at L1541.

## Coverage debt (planner to blueprint)
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso_eq_comp`** (L1248) — new helper, no blueprint block.
  Lean proof depends on `tensorObjIsoOfIso_trans`. Add a `\label`+`\lean`+`\uses` entry. (Sole new uncovered
  decl this iter; `archon dag-query unmatched` = 115, net −2 vs iter-055.)
- **Minor blueprint imprecision** (lvb): in `lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta` the δ-bridge display
  writes the source leg as `(pullbackValIso f (M⊗N)).inv` but the Lean uses
  `sheafificationCompPullback…hom.app`. Proof is `rfl` so content is unaffected — a blueprint-writer display
  fix, not a correctness issue.

## Do-NOT-retry / strategic guardrails (still live)
- Do NOT build `Functor.Monoidal (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ)` — globally FALSE (δ not iso for general
  modules, `Γ(ℙ¹,𝒪(1))=0`) and useless for the dual flank. The Cone A sheaf-transport route (this iter) is the
  correct shape; Cone B is bespoke. This was the iter-055 open question, RESOLVED in the iter-056 plan.
- No informal-agent API key is set (`DEEPSEEK/MOONSHOT/OPENROUTER/OPENAI/GEMINI` all empty) — bridge 3 has no
  external second-opinion channel; rely on effort-breaker + mathlib-analogist (read-only) instead.
